channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republicans and Democrats in Kansas agree that concerns about the economy drove voters to support President Donald Trump by a 16% margin. They also know that ads from Trump and others targeting transgender rights resonated with voters.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, JOHN HANNA and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump finds new ways to flex presidential power after returning to White House
item.description: President Donald Trump unleashed an unprecedented barrage of executive orders after taking office this week.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump defends pardons for rioters and suggests Proud Boys could have place in politics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on his first full day in office Tuesday defended his decision to grant clemency to people convicted of assaulting police officers during the 2021 attack on the Capitol and suggested there could be a place in American politics for the Proud Boys and Oath Keepe
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Coast Guard surging assets to Gulf of America, other waterways to support Trump’s executive orders
item.description: In support of President Trump's executive orders, the Coast Guard will send additional resources to maritime borders, the military branch said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: Senators vetting the nomination of Minnesotan Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Republican senators issue warning to historic museums ahead of pro-life march: ‘Respect’ free speech
item.description: Sens. Cruz and Young urge D.C. museums to respect First Amendment rights of pro-life protesters after Smithsonian kicked out students in 2023 for wearing pro-life gear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants at Texas border in shock after Trump canceled their asylum appointments
item.description: Hours after Trump’s inauguration, his administration canceled appointments allowing migrants to enter the U.S. to request asylum, leaving many of them stranded on the U.S.-Mexico border.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Uriel J. García And Alejandro Serrano, KPRC 2

item.title: Key career officials at Justice Department reassigned to different positions, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has reassigned key senior officials across multiple divisions as part of a leadership shakeup ahead of the expected confirmation of President Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, Pam Bondi, multiple people familiar with the matter said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump set to visit NC, California, Nevada
item.description: The president said he will be going Friday to North Carolina, which he said “has been abandoned by the Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats struggle to pick their message against Trump's shock-and-awe campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Democrats knew this was coming. President Donald Trump promised a shock-and-awe campaign to deliver major policy victories immediately after he took office. Much of it was outlined in the Project 2025 document that Democrats predicted he would adopt.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 states sue to block illegal migrants from census count used to assign congressional seats, electoral votes
item.description: Louisiana, Ohio, Kansas and West Virginia sued to block illegal immigrants from being counted when apportioning congressional seats and electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis cites ‘Gulf of America’ in winter storm order after Trump rebranding
item.description: Florida's governor appeared to take President Donald Trump's cue and reference the "Gulf of America" in his latest executive order.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration ousts Linda Fagan, first female Coast Guard commandant
item.description: Adm. Kevin Lunday, the former vice commandant of the Coast Guard, will take over as acting commandant, the statement notes, offering no other details of the switch.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pumps brakes on Day 1 tariffs
item.description: Trump campaigned hard on tariffs, which allowed him to tap into undercurrents of frustration about the globalized economy.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Cannon blocks DOJ from sharing Smith's Mar-a-Lago report with lawmakers
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon blocked the Justice Department from sharing with four top lawmakers the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report reviewing his investigation into President Trump’s mishandling of classified records.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Cheney responds to Trump: 'Neither lies nor the liar' age well
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) blasted President Trump for false statements he made about the now-disbanded Jan. 6 committee, calling them a “reminder that neither lies nor the liar who tells them get better with age.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Taliban announce release of two Americans held in Afghanistan in a prisoner exchange
item.description: Afghanistan's Taliban government announced on Tuesday the release of two Americans in a prisoner exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump pardons nearly all Jan. 6 rioters on first day of presidency
item.description: President Trump pardoned nearly all Jan. 6 defendants on Monday, making good on a long-standing campaign promise just hours into his second presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Ratcliffe moves closer to CIA chief confirmation
item.description: John Ratcliffe took a step closer to being confirmed to lead the CIA on Monday as the Senate Intelligence Committee advanced his nomination. The panel advanced his nomination 14 to 3 shortly after President Trump was sworn into office. All three "no" votes were cast by Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Republicans express optimism, state Democrat chair urges caution after inauguration
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) – Many Florida Republicans in the state legislature traveled to the nation’s capital for Inauguration Day events. Lawmakers like Sen. Rick Scott who works on Capitol Hill posted a video statement on X congratulating President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Expert: Trump can’t override Minnesota state law with executive orders
item.description: University of Minnesota political science professor Kathryn Pearson says President Donald Trump can’t direct states in matters that are up to state law.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Texas could join Louisiana with a law to require the Ten Commandments in classrooms
item.description: Similar proposals are in multiple states after a court ruling in 2022, though opponents say the move would impose one religious view on people with different religious traditions
item.dc:creator: Andrew Schneider / Houston Public Media, MPR News

item.title: Trump reinforces use of his 'Remain in Mexico' policy
item.description: The policy, which was created and implemented during the first Trump administration in 2019, resulted in tens of thousands of migrants waiting for extended periods in Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: Florida officials react to Trump inauguration
item.description: Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States on Monday. Here's how Florida officials are responding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to make historic move towards revoking birthright citizenship
item.description: Trump wants to reinterpret the phrasing of the 14th Amendment to mean that the federal government would not recognize automatic birthright citizenship for children born in the U.S. to parents without legal status.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump sworn in as 47th president, celebrates with supporters
item.description: On a frigid holiday Monday in Washington, D.C., Donald Trump took the oath of office for a second time to become the 47th president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, PIX 11

item.title: Trump to promise 'new era of national success' in inaugural address
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is set to promise a “new era of national success” in his inaugural address, according to The Wall Street Journal.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Lawler: Trump has right to revoke congestion pricing approval
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Congestion pricing began in New York City earlier this month. Under congestion pricing, drivers are charged a $9 toll during peak hours to enter Manhattan’s central business district at and below 60th Street. U.S. Rep.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pam Bondi grilled over Trump's influence at AG hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Pam Bondi, President-elect Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Justice, echoed attacks on the agency she would lead if confirmed, but demurred on how she might handle tests of its independence. Bondi is a more traditional pick to lead the department than Trump’s initial choice, former Rep.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis hopes incoming Trump administration will kickstart crackdown on illegal immigration
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis will be speaking in Winter Haven on Wednesday morning at the Polk County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
